era_context: Continuation of the Fourth Dynasty in Egypt under Khafre or his successor. The Akkadian Empire under Sargon continues consolidation and military expansion. The Harappan civilization maintains its mature urban phase. This year falls within a period of relative stability and monumental achievement in the major civilizations of the Old World.

events.title: Continuation of Fourth Dynasty Egypt, pyramid construction
events.description: The Fourth Dynasty continues its programme of monumental construction. Whether under Khafre or his successor, the Egyptian state maintains centralized control, resource mobilization for pyramid and temple construction, and administrative organization. The Nile flood cycle continues to structure agricultural and administrative calendars.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from Giza and other pyramid sites

events.certainty_note: No specific events are attested for this year. The broader context of Fourth Dynasty rule is confirmed, but year-specific precision is not available.

events.title: Akkadian military campaigns and administrative consolidation
events.description: Sargon of Akkad continues military campaigns to consolidate control over Mesopotamian territories. Administrative reforms promote the use of Akkadian language in official contexts. The empire's reach extends from the Persian Gulf to the Mediterranean coast. Military and administrative infrastructure supports the empire's expansion.
events.certainty_note: Sargon's reign is confirmed, but year-specific events for 2473 BCE are not attested. The broader pattern of military consolidation is documented.

events.title: Harappan trade networks and urban administration
events.description: The Harappan civilization maintains extensive trade networks with Mesopotamia, the Persian Gulf, and possibly Central Asia. Urban centres continue to function with standardized systems of weights, measures, and administrative organization. Archaeological evidence suggests sophisticated planning and resource management.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from Indus Valley sites
Mesopotamian cuneiform references to trade with Meluhha

events.certainty_note: The Harappan civilization's existence and trade networks are confirmed. Year-specific events are not attested.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are attested for 2473 BCE in any civilization. The broader historical context is confirmed, but year-specific precision is not available. Chronological uncertainty remains ±10–20 years for this period.
historiographic_note: For 2473 BCE, the historical record is even more limited than for 2474 BCE. No year-specific events are documented. The record consists of general patterns of political, military, and economic activity inferred from broader archaeological and textual evidence. Chronological precision is not achievable for this year.

graph_edges.note: Continuation of Fourth Dynasty rule and monumental construction
Continuation of Akkadian consolidation and expansion
